acceptance_targets: [CH17_PROSE_CLIPPED_FRAGMENT_TIC] In the revised versions of snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:3-5, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:47-49, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:67-69, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:195-199, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:213-215, every sentence is grammatically complete except isolated level-count callouts such as "Twelve." At least four of the cited clipped constructions are replaced with full sentences that preserve the same sensory facts and pressure.

[CH17_CRAFT_REPEATED_THRESHOLD_CERTIFICATION] After the revised version of snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:117, each remaining section must add at least one new dramatic element beyond the already-established fact that two contributors may fail: snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:135-177 must contain either a new decision with visible cost or a materially new fact, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:241-269 must end on a tactical change, loss, or irreversible consequence. The ending fails if it can still be paraphrased as another version of Osen's earlier warning.

[R18] Ch17 contains no more than 12 instances of the generic word 'stone/stones', with at least 4 geologically varied alternatives used.
[LW08-004] A reader can say when Sable learned the three-contributor information and no longer has to invent a reason she omitted it during the farmhouse warning.

[LW09-002] By the middle of chapter 18, the reader should know the threshold is unstable and that Sable is nearby, but should not feel able to predict with certainty that a seven-level third-contributor save is the only remaining route.
[length_chapter_17_min_words] Chapter word count is >= 6000 while preserving heading contract.

findings.acceptance_test: In the revised versions of snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:3-5, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:47-49, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:67-69, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:195-199, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:213-215, every sentence is grammatically complete except isolated level-count callouts such as "Twelve." At least four of the cited clipped constructions are replaced with full sentences that preserve the same sensory facts and pressure.
findings.problem: The chapter repeatedly falls into clipped, article-stripped narration patterns ("Not a gust but a condition," "A wound," "Not shouted. Carried.," "Tight turns. Worn stone.," "Not night. Underground dark.," "No way back.") that read like mechanical punch-up rather than controlled close-third compression. Because the constitution explicitly forbids elliptical clipping in narration, the recurrence stops feeling like pressure-sharpened style and starts feeling like autopilot.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the fragment-heavy spans at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:3-5, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:47-49, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:67-69, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:195-199, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:213-215 into grammatically complete sentences that stay inside Kem's perception. Keep the compression, heat, and shock, but remove subjectless/article-stripped scaffolds unless the line is an isolated level count.

findings.acceptance_test: After the revised version of snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:117, each remaining section must add at least one new dramatic element beyond the already-established fact that two contributors may fail: snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:135-177 must contain either a new decision with visible cost or a materially new fact, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:241-269 must end on a tactical change, loss, or irreversible consequence. The ending fails if it can still be paraphrased as another version of Osen's earlier warning.
findings.problem: Osen's briefing lands the chapter's major turn cleanly, but the back half keeps re-certifying the same point that two contributors are no longer enough. Vel and Kem restate it, the Sable intercut certifies it again with the report language, and the chapter ends on Osen repeating the warning. That produces structural recurrence and overstatement where later beats feel like confirmation of the reveal instead of a fresher escalation built from it.
findings.rewrite_direction: Once the reveal has landed in snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:97-117, compress the explanatory restatement in snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:135-161 and rework snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:165-177 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:241-269 so each section buys new dramatic work rather than another phrasing of the same arithmetic. Options that would solve it: let Sable's intercut pivot on a concrete risk, delay, or spend decision instead of mainly the remembered report; give the basin-floor sequence an irreversible tactical loss, injury, separation, or spent resource; or end on a changed operational fact rather than Osen restating that it is not enough.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch17 does not re-inventory Dren's daughter, age, treatment cost, or quote back 'my girl's my zero' from Ch16.
findings.evidence: Ch16 L3715: 'My girl's my zero... a bill for forty levels of bone work I can barely cover'; Ch17 L3927: 'Dren was somewhere north with a ruined left arm, a hundred and twenty levels, and an eight-year-old daughter waiting for a healer to make her legs hold. Vel kept seeing his face when he'd said my girl's my zero.'
findings.problem: Dren's motivational baseline (daughter Mira, leg treatment, economic pressure) is fully established through his farewell speech at Ch16 L3701-3749. Ch17 L3927 restates the same details one chapter later -- daughter, age, healer need, ruined arm, level count -- and quotes back 'my girl's my zero' verbatim. The reader received this information comprehensively in the previous chapter.
findings.rewrite_direction: At L3927, replace the summary-restatement with a compressed emotional reference. Vel can remember Dren without re-listing his daughter's age, condition, and the verbatim quote. One sentence of emotional residue is sufficient.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch17 contains no more than 12 instances of the generic word 'stone/stones', with at least 4 geologically varied alternatives used.
findings.evidence: The word 'stone' (including stones) appears 20 times in Ch17 (~132 prose lines, ~3,400 prose words). This is 1 occurrence per 6-7 lines. Manuscript-wide total is 113. The Scour basin is a stone landscape, but the word has become the default noun for every surface, feature, and obstacle.
findings.problem: Ch17 has 20 stone/stones references in 132 prose lines. While the Scour basin is geologically defined, using 'stone' for every surface flattens the landscape vocabulary. The manuscript-wide count of 113 suggests this is a pervasive default noun.
findings.rewrite_direction: Replace 8-10 of Ch17's weaker 'stone' instances with geologically specific terms (sandstone, shale, basalt, bedrock, ledge, outcrop, slab) or structural descriptions (wall, shelf, rib, fin) that are already present in the prose but underused.

findings.acceptance_test: A reader can say when Sable learned the three-contributor information and no longer has to invent a reason she omitted it during the farmhouse warning.
findings.cross_chapter_context: Ch15 shows Sable briefing the trio about the Red Reserve garrison and Council authorization, but she does not mention threshold degradation in that scene. The Ch17 revision must explain when and how Sable learned the specialist report about the degraded threshold — either from classified files she accessed after the farmhouse encounter, from intelligence gathered during her solo tracking, or from the Scour garrison's own records. Without this timestamp, her Ch15 omission reads as selective withholding rather than a gap in her knowledge.
findings.evidence: In the farmhouse, Sable frames her help as "There are two things you need before dark" and gives the Red Reserve and permanent garrison brief at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3616 and snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3626. Chapter 17 later reveals that she has access to a specialist report whose key line is the actual threshold condition at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4102.

evidence.excerpt: 3616: "There are two things you need before dark," Sable said. "First, the Council has now authorized the Red Reserve."
3626: "The second thing is older," Sable said. "The Red Reserve has kept a classified garrison at the Scour for years. Two operatives on permanent station. One commander in place for five."

4102: During the third week of pursuit she had opened files no one thought to hide from an auditor. Threshold assessment. Dampening operation. Specialist report. The key line sat in her memory with clerical precision: *Two-contributor cascade insufficient at current degradation parameters. Three-contributor minimum recommended for stable threshold event.*

findings.problem: Because the later chapter never places when Sable learned the specialist report, the boundary makes her chapter 15 briefing read selectively incomplete. On consecutive reading, the reader has to stop and ask whether she discovered this afterward or simply withheld the most important Scour fact.
findings.rewrite_direction: Timestamp the report discovery on the page. Either add a beat in chapter 15 or early chapter 17 that she has not yet cracked the threshold files, or show her realizing after the farmhouse that she failed to warn them about the degraded site.

findings.acceptance_test: By the middle of chapter 18, the reader should know the threshold is unstable and that Sable is nearby, but should not feel able to predict with certainty that a seven-level third-contributor save is the only remaining route.
findings.evidence: Osen already establishes that the damped site may no longer take two contributors and that extra energy is the only theoretical compensation (snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4028; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4044; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4048). The Sable intercut then states the exact report language that three contributors are required and tags Sable as the missing third (snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4100; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4102; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4104). Chapter 18 keeps re-signaling her arrival through the scree cues before she finally stabilizes the tear (snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4211; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4255; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4299; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4353).; Osen already tells Kem the site is degraded and that "more energy improves any mechanism" at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4028 and snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4044. The Sable intercut then states the exact answer; "Three-contributor minimum recommended for stable threshold event; " and reduces the situation to "Vel and Kem were two. Sable was three hours away" at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4102 and snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4104. Chapter 18 resolves the climax exactly that way when Sable's seven levels stabilize the tear at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4353.

evidence.excerpt: 4028: "Then listen carefully, because I am trying to stop you before anybody starts spending." Her arms dropped again. "The threshold has degraded."
4044: Osen gave him a long look. "More energy improves any mechanism. That is not the same thing as me briefing you through my perimeter."

4048: "There are theoretical compensations in a report I was never supposed to read. I'm not handing you a cleaner attempt. I'm giving you updated arithmetic so you stop walking toward bad odds and make me crack bones about it." She glanced toward the ridge. "If the woman up there believes two people can still do what happened here eight years ago, she keeps coming. If she knows the site was crippled on purpose, maybe she doesn't."

4100: Sable had been tracking for nine days. Two Perception spends had brought her here. Two hundred and thirty-six levels remained, enough for almost any official purpose and, if the report was right, enough to brace a damaged threshold.

4102: During the third week of pursuit she had opened files no one thought to hide from an auditor. Threshold assessment. Dampening operation. Specialist report. The key line sat in her memory with clerical precision: *Two-contributor cascade insufficient at current degradation parameters. Three-contributor minimum recommended for stable threshold event.*
4104: Vel and Kem were two. Sable was three hours away.

4211: High on the north scree, gravel hissed down in a narrow, steady ribbon. Too regular for wind. The commander looked that direction once and looked away. She kept every agent on the basin floor. Nobody could break from the line to investigate the ridge and still keep Vel and Kem off the point.
4255: High on the north scree, the ribbon of falling stone started again, lower now.

4299: The ribbon of gravel on the north wall had become a figure now, tiny but human, coming down the ugly route on purpose. The commander saw it too. She still did not turn an agent to deal with it. If she thinned the line for even a few seconds, Vel would have the point.
4353: Then seven fresh levels slammed into the cascade.

findings.problem: The window explains the mechanical solution to the climax before the climax begins, then reminds the reader several more times that the solution is on its way. That turns Sable's intervention from a live choice under pressure into execution of already-solved arithmetic, flattening suspense across the whole 17/18 run.
findings.rewrite_direction: Keep the degradation reveal and Sable's southward movement, but do not hand the reader the exact contributor formula or repeatedly confirm that the solution is approaching. Let Osen's warning create uncertainty rather than a complete equation. Cut or compress the Sable intercut's explicit three-contributor arithmetic and the repeated north-scree approach reminders so that Sable's arrival in Ch18 feels like a risky live choice under pressure rather than the execution of already-solved arithmetic. The reader should know the odds worsened but should not be able to predict the exact mechanical solution in a single sentence.

findings.acceptance_test: Chapter word count is >= 6000 while preserving heading contract.
findings.rewrite_direction: Add causally necessary scene material that strengthens the chapter engine, increases pressure, and lands the planned state shift without filler.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

Every narration sentence must be grammatically complete. Do not use article-stripped noun-phrase fragments, subjectless stubs, or telegraphic fragment strings as a rhythmic device. Isolated level-count callouts ('Twelve.' or 'Zero.') are acceptable; stacked fragment clusters ('Not night. Underground dark.' or 'Too slow. Too loud. Too late.') are not. Achieve compression through short complete sentences, not by dropping subjects and articles.

When a scene beat has landed through concrete image, action, dialogue, or bodily sensation, do not add an abstract summary sentence that restates its meaning in thesis language. If a paragraph's only job is to translate what the reader already understood from the prior image or line of dialogue, cut it or replace it with a new fact, consequence, or pressure. Trust the scene. One beat, one landing.

No character should deliver a multi-sentence thematic diagnosis, balanced mini-speech, or quotable aphorism during a pressured scene. Accusations, confessions, and revelations must arrive through messy, character-true speech: interruptions, self-corrections, false starts, incomplete grammar, practical redirects, and mid-sentence recalibrations. If a line of dialogue can be lifted as a standalone epigram, it is probably too composed for the scene pressure. Rhetorical questions used for emphasis rather than genuine inquiry are not permitted for Vel.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This is a dark comedy about a world where doing nothing is rational and doing something is self-destruction. Both the comedy and the darkness must be fully committed. Do not soften combat into choreography or spectacle — fights should hurt, cost real things, and leave damage. Do not soften the comedy into gentle satire — the absurdity of the Hoarded and their agents should be genuinely funny, sometimes cruelly so. Do not soften grief into sentimentality or revelation into epiphany. The premise demands a tonal register that holds absurdity and genuine pain in the same hand. When the material is uncomfortable — the physical reality of injury, the moral weight of exploitation, the specific grief of losing someone to a place you cannot reach — lean into specificity rather than retreating to safety. A draft that never risks anything produces a novel that never moves anyone.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism in combat, injury, consequence, or emotional extremity is a craft failure. If Vel breaks ribs, the reader should feel the break — the specific wrongness of breathing with cracked bone, the way she holds herself, the pain when she forgets. If Kem is terrified, the terror should be specific and physical: hands, stomach, breathing, the precise quality of his fear. Do not abstract away from embodied experience into summary or generalization.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for all characters except Sable (low) and early-book Kem (initially low, shifting to high). Uncontracted forms are reserved for deliberate emphasis, formal Hoarded speech contexts, or bureaucratic register. In casual conversation, unjustified full-form lines should be treated as suspect.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Vary chapter rhythm aggressively. Combat chapters should be compressed and physical. Discovery chapters should have more room for observation and the surprise of new information. Quiet chapters should breathe — longer scenes, more sensory detail, conversations that circle rather than drive. Intercut chapters (like Ch 11's Kem/Sable split and Ch 17's Sable intercut) should have contrasting textures between sections. No two consecutive chapters should have the same structural shape: vary scene count, dialogue density, pacing, and the ratio of action to interiority. Dark comedy chapters in the back half (Ch 12, 14, 15, 17) must commit fully to comic energy as a primary narrative mode: the absurdity of Hoarded bureaucratic language applied to human exploitation, the grim humor of fighting for your life with single-digit levels, the comedy of precision teamwork performed in emotional silence. These chapters carry genuine stakes alongside their comedy — the tonal fusion is the point, not tonal alternation.
prose_style_profile.diction: Concrete, physical, occasionally wry. Avoids both literary pretension and genre cliche. The world has its own vocabulary (Hoarded, Spent, Ledger, Stillness, the Scour, the Margins) and these terms should be used naturally, not defined in exposition. Metaphors earned from the world's own materials — spending, counting, weight, erosion — rather than imported from outside the setting. The Hoarded institutional register — bureaucratic classifications, procedural euphemism, administrative language applied to human exploitation — is a specific comic instrument in the Margins chapters. When rendering Hoarded documents, policies, or institutional speech, the language should be precise, procedural, and devastating in its indifference.
forbidden_drift_patterns: Narrator-as-comedian: comedy arises from situation and character, not from narrative commentary or winking asides to the reader
Ability-use described like video game tooltips: do not produce stat readouts or system notifications in prose
Emotional temperature permanently at maximum: not every scene is a crisis and not every internal moment is an epiphany
Generic fantasy description: landscape, architecture, and weather must be specific to this world's conditions, not recycled from the fantasy-prose commons
Competence porn: do not let characters be consistently impressive — they should fumble, miscalculate, and fail at things that are hard
Thematic underlining: the prose should not highlight its own meanings or signal when something is symbolically significant
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third-person limited, shifting with the POV character. Vel chapters are compressed and physical — the prose should feel like it is counting. Kem chapters are more expansive and curious — the prose has more room for observation and surprise. Sable chapters are structured and institutional, loosening as she changes. The POV break in Ch 13–14 (two consecutive Kem chapters) should make Vel's absence felt: the reader is locked inside Kem's betrayed perspective, and the prose should reflect the claustrophobia of a mind cycling through anger without access to the other person's interiority. The distance should be intimate enough to access characters' internal arithmetic but not so close it becomes stream-of-consciousness.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Varied by chapter texture and scene pressure. Short declarative sentences in combat. Longer, conditional, clause-heavy sentences in reflection. Comma-heavy run-ons in panic or exhilaration. The Ledger's arithmetic should bleed into prose rhythm during ability-use moments — characters counting, weighing, calculating. Quiet chapters should breathe. Action chapters should compress. Two consecutive chapters should not share the same dominant rhythm.
sensory_bias: proprioceptive awareness — the body's internal sense of position, strain, damage, and capacity
physical weight and effort — characters feel gravity, distance, fatigue
the Ledger's feedback — a character's awareness of their remaining levels as a persistent background sensation
environmental specificity — terrain, weather, architecture rendered through what a body encounters
